senses.definition: to develop a relationship with a person

examples.x: They bonded over their shared love for hiking and outdoor adventures.
Moving to a new city forced her to bond with her neighbors to build a sense of community.
Joining a book club allowed her to bond with fellow book lovers and discuss their favorite reads.

senses.definition: someone who helps or supports someone else in certain activities or against someone else

examples.x: She found a reliable ally in her coworker, who always supported her ideas during meetings.
He considered her his closest ally, always there to lend a helping hand in times of trouble.

senses.definition: a person whom one knows but is not a close friend

examples.x: She bumped into an old acquaintance at the grocery store and exchanged pleasantries.
Though they were only acquaintances, he always greeted her warmly whenever they crossed paths.
Networking events provide opportunities to meet new acquaintances in the professional world.
He invited his acquaintances from work to the party, hoping to expand his social circle.
It's always nice to catch up with acquaintances at social gatherings and hear about their recent experiences.

senses.definition: someone's best friend, ‌used by young people on social media, especially in text messages
a close friend

examples.x: James and David have been best buddies since they were kids, always looking out for each other.
Hey buddy, do you mind lending me a hand with this heavy box?
Walking down the street, he bumped into his old high school buddy and they reminisced about their teenage years.
Fishing trips are always more enjoyable when you have your buddies with you to share the experience.
At the company picnic, employees brought their families along, creating a relaxed atmosphere where coworkers could mingle and get to know each other as buddies outside of work.

senses.definition: a friend

examples.x: After a long day at work, Tom likes to unwind with a cold beer and chat with his pals.
She introduced her new rescue dog as her furry pal who never fails to brighten her day.
Walking through the park, he bumped into his old pal from college and they caught up on each other's lives.
The kids down the street are always playing together; they're the best of pals.

senses.definition: a person or animal with which one travels or spends a lot of time

examples.x: During her solo backpacking trip through Europe, she made many new companions along the way.
His loyal dog has been his constant companion for over a decade, providing comfort and companionship.
The elderly man found a new companion in his neighbor's kitten after his own cat passed away.
Traveling alone can be daunting, so she decided to bring her sister along as her companion on the trip.
He enjoys going on long hikes in the mountains with his canine companion, exploring new trails together.

senses.definition: a romantic or sexual partner, especially in a long-term or committed relationship

examples.x: She is my life mate, and we share everything together.
He considers his wife not just his mate, but also his best friend and confidante.
In Australian culture, it's common to refer to one's spouse as "mate," emphasizing the equality and partnership in the relationship.
After a long day at work, he looks forward to coming home to his loving mate and children.
She found her perfect mate in her college sweetheart, and they've been inseparable ever since.

senses.definition: a person who shares the responsibilities of raising a child

examples.x: Sarah and Tom are dedicated co-parents, sharing the responsibilities of raising their children equally.
Despite living apart, they remain committed co-parents, collaborating on important decisions for their child's upbringing.
As co-parents, they prioritize open communication and mutual respect in their shared parenting duties.
Despite their divorce, they strive to be supportive co-parents, attending their child's school events and activities together.
The co-parents work together to create a loving and nurturing environment for their children, despite their differences.

senses.definition: a brother that shares only one biological parent with one

examples.x: My half-brother
Even though we have different mothers, my half-brother
After my parents' divorce, I gained a half-brother when my dad remarried.
Despite being half-brothers, we've always treated each other like full siblings.
Growing up, I didn't see my half-brother very often because he lived with his mom in another city.

senses.definition: a sister that shares only one biological parent with one

examples.x: My half-sister and I share the same mother but have different fathers.
Even though we're half-sisters, we've always considered each other family.
Growing up, my half-sister and I had a complicated relationship, but we've become closer as adults.
It was a shock to discover I had a half-sister, but I'm grateful for the bond we've formed.
Despite the age gap, my half-sister has always looked out for me like a big sister.

senses.definition: someone who has the legal right to inherit the property, money, or title of a deceased individual

examples.x: As the eldest son, he is the rightful heir to his father's estate.
The lawyer contacted the heirs to inform them of their inheritance from their late aunt.
The king's only heir to the throne is his daughter, who will succeed him as queen.
Without a will, the deceased's assets will be distributed among their heirs according to state law.
She was surprised to learn that she was the sole heir to her distant relative's vast fortune.

senses.definition: one's closest living relative or relatives

examples.x: In case of emergency, the hospital will contact your next of kin listed on your medical records.
The police notified the deceased's next of kin about the accident.
She designated her sister as her next of kin in case anything happened to her.
The soldier's commanding officer informed his next of kin about his heroic actions in battle.
As the next of kin, you will be responsible for making decisions regarding the deceased's estate.

senses.definition: a child whose parents have died

examples.x: The orphan found solace and a new family in the orphanage after losing both parents in a tragic accident.
The community rallied together to support the orphan, providing financial assistance and emotional support.
She was determined to adopt an orphan and give them a loving home and family.
The orphanage provides a safe haven for orphans, offering them shelter, education, and care.
The orphan's resilience and strength inspired those around them, despite facing unimaginable loss at a young age.

senses.definition: someone who shares the same blood with a specific person who lived many years ago

examples.x: The historian traced her family's lineage back to a prominent historical figure, claiming to be a direct descendant.
As a descendant of immigrants, she felt a strong connection to her ancestors' homeland and culture.
The DNA test confirmed that she was a descendant of the famous explorer, much to her surprise.
The ancient artifact was passed down through generations, eventually ending up in the hands of a direct descendant.

senses.definition: (of a child or parent) related through adoption

examples.x: Despite not being biologically related, the adoptive family created a strong bond through love and shared experiences.
The adoptive mother attended parenting classes to learn how to best support her child's emotional and developmental needs.
The adoptive father proudly watched his daughter graduate from college, grateful for the opportunity to be part of her journey.
The adoptive siblings may not share DNA, but their bond is just as strong as any biological family's.

senses.definition: representing or involving members of two different races

examples.x: The biracial child proudly embraces both sides of their cultural heritage, celebrating diversity and inclusivity.
The biracial couple faced prejudice and discrimination from society but remained resilient in their love and commitment to each other.
As a biracial individual, he advocates for greater understanding and acceptance of multiracial identities in society.
The biracial family celebrates cultural traditions from both sides of their heritage, fostering a sense of belonging and pride.
Biracial representation in media and literature is essential for promoting diversity and challenging stereotypes.

senses.definition: (of two people, particularly in the same family) older

examples.x: The elder sister always looked out for her younger siblings, offering guidance and support.
As the elder sibling, she felt a sense of responsibility to set a good example for her younger brother.
The elder daughter took on more responsibilities around the house to help her parents with household chores.
Growing up, he looked up to his elder brother as a role model and mentor.
She looked up to her elder sister for advice and guidance throughout her life.

senses.definition: (of people) having a very close relationship

examples.x: They shared an intimate moment under the stars, revealing their deepest thoughts.
She cherished the intimate bond she shared with her best friend, knowing she could confide in them about anything.
They shared an intimate hug, expressing their love and affection for each other.
She confided in her intimate friend about her deepest fears and dreams.
Their intimate relationship allowed them to be vulnerable and honest with each other.

senses.definition: characteristic of a sister or like one

examples.x: She offered her sisterly advice when her friend was going through a tough time.
Their sisterly bond was evident in the way they laughed and shared secrets with each other.
She gave him a sisterly hug to comfort him after his breakup.
Despite their occasional disagreements, they always shared a strong sense of sisterly affection.
She admired her sister's sisterly qualities, such as her kindness and generosity towards others.

senses.definition: (of a family or group of people) having a strong and friendly relationship with each other

examples.x: The residents of the small town formed a tight-knit community, always willing to lend a helping hand to their neighbors.
Despite moving away for college, she remained connected to her tight-knit group of childhood friends.
The company fostered a tight-knit work culture, promoting collaboration and camaraderie among employees.
Growing up in a tight-knit family taught him the value of loyalty and support.
The tight-knit group of volunteers worked tirelessly to improve their local community.

senses.definition: the people that a person is descended from

examples.x: She traced her ancestry back to the 1700s, discovering that her ancestors were early settlers in the region.
His diverse ancestry includes Irish, Italian, and Native American roots.
Many people are interested in learning about their ancestry to understand their family's history and heritage.
He took a DNA test to uncover more details about his ancestry and found relatives he didn't know he had.
The festival celebrated the rich ancestry of the local community, highlighting traditions and customs passed down through generations.

senses.definition: an individual's religious or ethnic background that is passed down to them from their ancestors

examples.x: She takes great pride in her cultural heritage and often participates in traditional ceremonies and festivals.
His heritage includes a mix of Scandinavian and Eastern European traditions, which he celebrates with his family.
Understanding one's heritage can provide a deeper sense of identity and belonging.
The museum exhibit focused on the rich heritage of the indigenous people, showcasing their art, music, and history.
She learned traditional recipes from her grandmother, preserving her culinary heritage for future generations.

senses.definition: a subdivision or a group of members of an extended family sharing the same ancestors

examples.x: The reunion brought together every branch of the extended family, some of whom hadn't seen each other in decades.
The Smith family has a long lineage, with each branch tracing back to a common ancestor who immigrated in the 1800s.
Each branch of the family contributed stories and photographs to the comprehensive family history book.
The genealogist discovered that their branch of the family had migrated westward during the early 20th century.
Although they belonged to different branches of the family, the cousins maintained close relationships throughout their lives.

senses.definition: the relationship between two or more brothers
a large group of people who are related to each other

examples.x: The entire clan gathered for the annual family reunion, filling the park with laughter and shared memories.
As the eldest member of the clan, she held a place of honor and respect during family gatherings.
Each summer, the clan traveled to their ancestral homeland to reconnect with their roots and heritage.
The clan's history was passed down through generations, with stories and traditions that strengthened their familial bond.
The wedding was a grand event, attended by members of the clan from all over the country.

senses.definition: the end of a relationship or an association

examples.x: The sudden breakup of their ten-year marriage shocked all their friends and family.
After the band's breakup, the members pursued solo careers and different musical projects.
She took a long vacation to help herself heal after the painful breakup with her longtime partner.
The company's breakup was inevitable after years of internal conflict and declining profits.
The breakup of the partnership left both entrepreneurs free to explore new business opportunities independently.

senses.definition: what one receives upon a person's death, such as property, money, etc.

examples.x: After her aunt passed away, she was surprised to learn that she had been left a substantial inheritance.
He used part of his inheritance to pay off his student loans and invested the rest in a new business venture.
The inheritance included a beautiful old house, several acres of land, and a collection of antique furniture.
Despite the significant inheritance he received, he chose to continue living a modest and simple life.
The siblings were in dispute over their father's inheritance, especially concerning the division of the family estate and other assets.

senses.definition: ‌the process of raising or taking care of one's child or children

examples.x: Parenting can be challenging, but it is also one of the most rewarding experiences in life.
They attended a parenting workshop to learn new strategies for managing their toddler's behavior.
Effective parenting involves setting boundaries while also showing unconditional love and support.
His parenting style emphasizes open communication and fostering independence in his children.

senses.definition: strong love and support expressed for a person or thing

examples.x: Sarah's unwavering devotion to her family was evident in her tireless efforts to support them through every challenge they faced.
The monk's daily prayers and meditation rituals were a testament to his profound religious devotion.
Mark's devotion to his art led him to spend countless hours in his studio, perfecting every brushstroke to achieve his vision.
The dog's unwavering devotion to its owner was evident as it stood guard by the door, eagerly awaiting their return.
Jennifer's philanthropic devotion was showcased through her tireless efforts in organizing charity events and fundraisers for local causes in need.

senses.definition: a close relationship in which there is a good understanding and communication between people

examples.x: Despite meeting for the first time, their shared interests and easy conversation quickly established a strong rapport between them.
The teacher worked hard to build rapport with each student in the classroom, creating a supportive learning environment where everyone felt valued and understood.
Developing rapport with clients is crucial in sales; it helps to establish trust and understanding, ultimately leading to successful transactions.
Through active listening and empathy, the therapist built a rapport with her patients, creating a safe space for them to explore their emotions and experiences.
Team-building activities are often used in workplaces to strengthen rapport among employees, fostering collaboration and synergy in achieving common goals.

senses.definition: lasting the whole of a person's life

examples.x: His lifelong passion for music began when he was a child.
She formed a lifelong friendship with her childhood neighbor.
The lifelong commitment of marriage is a significant milestone for many couples.
He pursued a lifelong dream of traveling the world after retiring.
The organization aims to provide lifelong learning opportunities for adults.

senses.definition: to end a relationship that one was romantically involved in, often in a way that is unexpected or unfair

examples.x: After months of dating, Sarah was shocked when her boyfriend suddenly decided to dump her via text message.
Tom chose to dump his girlfriend without warning, leaving her devastated and questioning their entire relationship.
Emily felt betrayed when her partner chose to dump her for someone else.
James regretted the way he chose to dump his long-term partner, realizing later that he should have been more considerate.

senses.definition: a very friendly and affectionate relationship that two men have with each other, which is not sexual at all

examples.x: Despite their outwardly competitive nature, their friendship was deepened by an undeniable bromance, characterized by mutual respect and support.
The movie portrayed the heartwarming bromance between two unlikely friends, showcasing their bond through humor, loyalty, and shared experiences.
John and Mark's bromance was the talk of the office, with their colleagues admiring their close friendship and camaraderie.
The sitcom humorously depicted the ups and downs of a group of friends' bromances, highlighting the importance of male bonding and support.
Through their shared love of sports and late-night gaming sessions, Tim and Mike's bromance blossomed into a lifelong friendship, built on trust and understanding.

senses.definition: a close friend or companion from one's neighborhood or hometown, often sharing a similar background or upbringing

examples.x: Growing up in the same neighborhood, Carlos and Miguel were more than just friends; they were practically brothers, sharing the bond of being homeboys.
Despite their different paths in life, Juan never forgot his roots, always staying true to his homeboys and the community where he grew up.
Whenever things got tough, Maria knew she could count on her homeboys from the block to have her back, no questions asked.
Even after moving away for college, David remained close with his homeboys from high school, regularly keeping in touch and visiting whenever he could.
The local rap group's lyrics often paid homage to their homeboys and the struggles they faced growing up in the inner city, resonating with many listeners who shared similar experiences.
